description: A well-known Thai stir-fried noodle dish typically made with rice noodles, a tamarind-based sauce, egg, bean sprouts, and peanuts, often with shrimp, chicken, or tofu.
preparation_steps: Soak or prepare the rice noodles until pliable.
Make or combine the sauce, usually balancing sour, salty, sweet, and savory elements.
Stir-fry aromatics and protein if using.
Add noodles, sauce, and egg, then toss quickly over high heat.
Finish with bean sprouts, peanuts, chives, and serve with lime.

nutrition.protein: Moderate; higher with shrimp, chicken, or tofu
nutrition.fat: Low to moderate, but can be higher depending on oil and peanuts

health_advice.benefits: Can provide a mix of carbohydrates, protein, and some vegetables in one dish.
health_advice.risks: Can be high in sodium and added sugar depending on the sauce; portions can also be large.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Use more vegetables, moderate the sauce, and avoid excessive oil or sugar.

dietary_notes.note: Vegetarian and vegan versions are possible if fish sauce and egg are omitted or replaced. Gluten-free suitability depends on the sauces used.

allergens: Soy if soy sauce is used
